title: The World's Most Toxic Town: The Terrible Legacy of Zambia's Lead Mines | Pulitzer Center
impact_evidence: Environmental | Land, Soil & Geology | Topsoil degradation and loss of soil fertility -> "The fumes from the giant state-owned smelter, which closed in 1994, has left the dusty soil in the surrounding area with extreme levels of lead." | "In affected townships, the lead in soils is about 10 times the US safety limit and far higher in hotspots." || Environmental | Land, Soil & Geology | Tailings generation and management -> "new dangers are emerging as desperately poor people scavenge in the vast slag heap known as Black Mountain." | "Illegal miners scavenge for lead on Black Mountain, a huge slag heap, exposing themselves to extreme levels of lead." | "in another part of the mine waste dump, beyond a long breeze block wall..." || Environmental | Water Resources | Water management challenges -> "there is a now a fund of 16m kwacha (about $1.7m) that will be spent on cleaning up Kabwe's toxic pollution, providing the drug therapies that have been absent so far and repairing the clogged canal that is supposed to channel away the run-off from the mine site." || Environmental | Air & Climate | Dust generation -> "Children playing with bottle tops in the dust in Chowa township in Kabwe. The dust is highly contaminated with lead, which is extremely toxic." | "The dust sparkles with the metallic glint of galena – pure lead sulphide – and the soil right next to her four-year-old son, Acili, measures an astronomical 37,900ppm" || Social | Health, Safety & Well-being | Community exposure to dust, noise, pollution -> "Kabwe is the world's most toxic town, according to pollution experts, where mass lead poisoning has almost certainly damaged the brains and other organs of generations of children – and where children continue to be poisoned every day." | "The blood levels of lead in children in Kabwe are also known to be very high - a recent study revealed that every one of 246 children tested were above the safety limit of 5 micrograms per decilitre of blood." || Social | Health, Safety & Well-being | Accidents and fatalities (including structural failures such as tailings dams) -> ""When you don't make them properly, you find they just bury someone," says Provost Musonda, a young father of three, and people have died in the scarred hellscape of Black Mountain." || Social | Health, Safety & Well-being | Worker health and safety risks -> "On Black Mountain, bare-foot and ragged-clothed men dig out lead from the huge slag heap, often in long, unsupported tunnels, dug with hand tools and lit only by candles." | "Illegal miners scavenge for lead on Black Mountain, a huge slag heap, exposing themselves to extreme levels of lead." || Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Income generation and poverty reduction or exacerbation -> "He earns about 80 kwacha ($8.50) a day, unless his chest pains prevent him working. "If I could get another job, I would go there. But there is no way of sustaining our lives otherwise."" | "Women and children crush rocks to sell as gravel among the highly contaminated spoil heaps of the former mine." || Social | Living Conditions & Social Fabric | Education and training opportunities -> "The problem is they are not really learning well in school, so the lead is still affecting them," she says." | "we consider the children our future leaders and if they do not get a good education, they will not be capable." || Governance | Transparency & Accountability | Monitoring and disclosure of environmental and social performance -> "Lead poisoning remains a highly sensitive issue in Kabwe and people from several organisations refused to speak to the Guardian, while those trying to tackle the problem complain that data gathered by officials is not made public." || Governance | Risk Management & Long-Term Stewardship | Tailings and waste governance -> "More than 120 homes have had the soil in their yards replaced with clean soil from elsewhere." | "there is a now a fund of 16m kwacha (about $1.7m) that will be spent on cleaning up Kabwe's toxic pollution, providing the drug therapies that have been absent so far and repairing the clogged canal that is supposed to channel away the run-off from the mine site."
